title: Agility Robotics opens a 60,000-square-foot Fremont facility down the road from Tesla
subtitle: The Digit-maker plants a physical AI hub in Silicon Valley, one exit from where Tesla is preparing Optimus production.
author: AESOP AI Engine
body: Agility Robotics announced on July 16, 2026 — with TechCrunch confirming site details on July 17 — that it has opened a 60,000-square-foot Physical AI development hub in Fremont, California, one exit up the highway from the Tesla factory that is being retooled for Optimus. Agility plans to hire nearly 200 people at the new site across AI, machine learning, software engineering and field operations. The facility complements the company's RoboFab manufacturing operations in Salem, Oregon.

The point of the Fremont site is training, not manufacturing. It is where Agility's humanoid, Digit, will be taught new skills before being deployed to customers. Locating that work directly across the road from Tesla's Optimus line is a deliberate talent-recruitment move: the two companies will now be sharing the same commute, the same lunch spots and the same candidate pool.

The commercial context is that humanoids have moved past the demo era. Agility has active deployments with Schaeffler, GXO, Toyota Motor Manufacturing Canada, Mercado Libre and Amazon, and reports more than $300 million in multi-year orders for Digit v5 with a pipeline of over 30 customers. Figure AI is ramping Figure 03 at BMW's Spartanburg plant. Tesla is targeting a late-July to August Optimus Gen 3 low-volume production start at Fremont. Three plausible winners are training against each other inside a five-mile radius.

For learners interested in physical AI: the interesting work is no longer just in simulation or vision-language-action model papers. It is in the training loops that turn a general-purpose humanoid into a machine that reliably picks the right tote at Amazon or the right subassembly at Toyota. Skills in reinforcement learning, sim-to-real transfer, teleoperation data collection and warehouse operations research are now directly hireable at multiple companies within one zip code.
